name: Western Union
description_short: At 417 Broadway E in Seattle, WA 98102, Western Union provides check cashing and money transfer services. Call +1 206-328-6920 for details.
description_long:
Western Union is one of the world's largest money transfer companies, offering financial services through a distributed network of agent locations rather than traditional brick-and-mortar branches. The company has been facilitating cross-border and domestic money movement for decades, with a presence in virtually every US state through partnerships with major retailers like Walmart, Safeway, City Market, Kroger, and Circle K convenience stores.

Western Union's core services include sending money domestically and internationally, enabling recipients to pick up cash at agent locations, tracking transfers in real-time, paying bills online, processing money orders, and providing mobile top-up services. The company also offers Western Union Rewards loyalty programs, prepaid card services, and the ability to receive money directly to a card. Customers can initiate transfers online 24/7 through WU.com and complete payment at participating agent locations during their hours of operation, which typically range from early morning until 8-11 PM depending on the retail partner.

What distinguishes Western Union is its extensive agent network embedded within everyday retail locations, making cash pickup convenient for millions of Americans without requiring a traditional bank account. The company's 24/7 online initiation paired with flexible in-store completion timing across multiple retail partners creates accessibility that specialized financial institutions cannot match. Their multi-language support (English, Spanish, Haitian Creole, Chinese, French, Vietnamese) reflects service to diverse immigrant and international communities.

However, Western Union is primarily a money transfer and payment facilitation service rather than a traditional financial institution. Customers should expect fees for transfers and services, which the company encourages users to estimate before committing. The "Orange wallet" service was temporarily unavailable at the time of this profile, suggesting occasional service disruptions. Western Union is best suited for consumers needing immediate cash access, international remittances, or bill payment rather than those seeking ongoing banking relationships or credit-building products.
pros: 150+ agent locations across the US embedded in major retail chains (Walmart, Safeway, City Market) for convenient access
24/7 online money transfer initiation through WU.com with flexible in-store pickup during agent hours
Real-time transfer tracking available to monitor payment status
Multi-language support in 6 languages (English, Spanish, Haitian Creole, Chinese, French, Vietnamese)
Multiple service options including money orders, bill payment, mobile top-up, and card-based money receipt
No requirement for bank account ownership to send or receive money
Western Union Rewards loyalty program with referral incentives
cons: Service fees apply to transfers and money orders; company directs users to estimate prices before committing
Dependent on retail partner hours of operation (typically 8-11 PM closing); not truly 24/7 for cash pickup despite online availability
Technology disruptions evident (Orange wallet service was temporarily unavailable during profile creation)
Primarily a transaction processor rather than a financial institution; no credit-building, savings accounts, or lending products
Limited transparency on fee structure within the website content provided
best_for: Immigrants and international communities sending remittances to family abroad or receiving international transfers
Unbanked and underbanked consumers without traditional bank accounts needing to send/receive cash quickly
Individuals requiring immediate bill payment or money order services during non-traditional banking hours
Consumers seeking convenient cash access through familiar retail locations without visiting a separate storefront
diagnosis: Western Union is best for unbanked or underbanked consumers, immigrants sending international remittances, and anyone needing quick cash access or bill payment through convenient retail locations. The main caveat is that this is a transaction-based service with associated fees—not a banking relationship or credit-building product—and cash pickup availability depends on retail partner hours rather than true 24/7 access.
services: Domestic money transfer with online initiation
International money transfer and remittance services
Cash pickup at 150+ agent retail locations
